element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns movement in water environment
Core interaction mechanic introduced early with weak aquatic creatures
Players learn resource management through enemy meat consumption
Strategic evolution choices become central to progression
Transition from aquatic to terrestrial platforming

element_introduction_order.element: Tail swings and advanced attack abilities

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mandatory confrontations requiring specific evolved adaptations

element_introduction_order.element: Crystal collectibles (hints, EVO grants, temporary transformations)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional discovery mechanic; temporary power-ups provide strategic advantages

element_introduction_order.element: Health restoration (meat, plant life, selective evolution)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn resource management for sustainability
Meta-game feature for experimentation and build diversity
Significant biological shift requiring new survival strategies
Final form; apex of evolutionary path

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Each era has unique corrupted boss
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Narrative revelation: external force sabotaging natural evolution
Ultimate challenge; true antagonist identity revealed

typical_relief_triggers: Successful evolution upgrade grants temporary invulnerability moment
Completion of level (return to map screen)
Crystal bonuses and temporary transformation invincibility periods
typical_tension_triggers: Health bar depletion from enemy contact
Encounter with boss character at level end
Entry into new geological era (unfamiliar enemy types)
Loss of half EVO points upon death (cost of respawn)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating pressure across five geological eras; each era introduces new enemy types and required evolutionary adaptations. Tension builds within levels toward mandatory boss confrontations. Relief arrives through evolution progression and health restoration mechanics. Respawn penalty (50% EVO loss) creates consequence-driven pacing without permanent failure.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Crystals scattered throughout levels provide hints, EVO grants, and temporary transformations; rewarding exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Cambrian/Ordovician (fish form): gentle introduction to swimming, basic combat with weak aquatic enemies, abundant meat drops for EVO farming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Carboniferous to early Mesozoic: transition to land-based locomotion, introduction of jumping, moderate enemy variety, boss encounters require deliberate evolution planning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mesozoic (dinosaurs) through Quaternary (ice age/early man): mammalian evolution introduces new constraints, sparse healing resources, ice age mammals face remaining dinosaurs, humanoid form requires complete tactical rethinking
notable_spikes: Transition from aquatic to terrestrial (Carboniferous): requires mastery of new movement mechanics
Age of Dinosaurs (Mesozoic): powerful enemies demand significant evolved adaptations
Ice Age (Neogene): mammalian evolution creates biological weakness phase requiring re-specialization
Final confrontation with Bolbox: requires evolved human form with accumulated power

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of five geological periods has its own map screen divided into multiple levels. Each era introduces period-specific enemies, environmental hazards, and evolutionary pressures. Boss encounter at era culmination gates progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Cambrian Period (The World Before Land) vs. Mesozoic Era (Age of Dinosaurs)

notable_level_patterns.description: New abilities and body part improvements unlock new traversal and combat options. Levels implicitly encourage specific evolutionary paths through enemy design and environmental design.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tail swing ability critical for dinosaur combat; jumping upgrades necessary for land navigation

notable_level_patterns.description: Each era concludes with mandatory boss encounter significantly stronger than regular enemies. Bosses are crystal-corrupted species unique to that era, requiring accumulated evolution investment to defeat.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter-ending dinosaur boss; final Bolbox encounter in Eden

notable_level_patterns.description: Enemy meat drops and plant life availability vary by era, creating changing survival conditions. Late game (Ice Age, Early Man) features sparser healing resources, increasing pressure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Abundant aquatic food sources in Cambrian vs. scarce resources in Ice Age

sources_used: Wikipedia - E.V.O.: Search for Eden (gameplay, plot, development sections)
The Cutting Room Floor - E.V.O.: Search for Eden (regional differences, debug data)
